decision_tree.description: Pick the right testing strategy for your project

answer.pick: Test pyramid + heavy integration + contract testing + chaos
answer.rationale: Unit tests for logic, contract tests (Pact) for service boundaries, integration tests for adapters, chaos tests (Chaos Monkey) for resilience. Mutation testing to verify test quality.
Hypothesis / fast-check for property tests. Reference implementation in another language for cross-check. Fuzzing (AFL, libFuzzer) for parsers.

answer.pick: Test pyramid: many unit, fewer integration, few e2e
answer.rationale: 70% unit, 20% integration, 10% e2e. Use Playwright for e2e. Component tests with Testing Library. Mock at the boundary, not deep in the stack.
Pure unit for business logic. Integration tests for the full request/response cycle. Contract tests for inter-service calls. Postman/Newman for smoke.
API surface must be exhaustively tested. Property-based for input space. Golden files for serialization. Compatibility tests for old versions.
A 'does it boot' test. Move fast. Refactor when promoting to production.

answer.pick: Test pyramid: many unit, few e2e
answer.rationale: Start with the test pyramid. Add tests as features stabilize. Don't let the test debt accumulate.
